reviews.review_description:
Jupiter & Mars is pure joy for my mermaid heart. As a certified diver and someone who does “mermaid training” in real life with lap swimming and water resistance workouts, navigating the ocean as a dolphin felt like coming home. The mission—freeing sea creatures from human-made debris—carries a gentle, important message, while the gameplay flows naturally once you learn to move like your dolphin.

From the breathtaking underwater worlds to heartwarming moments (like helping a baby orca rescue its mom), every quest feels soothing, purposeful, and magical. It’s easy to follow, not overly complicated, and perfect for all ages. I would recommend this not only to ocean lovers, but also to our patients at The Drake Center who want a peaceful, non-violent VR journey that uplifts the spirit. I’m really looking forward to what these developers create next!

#11Mermaiddancer
Love swimming around saving and helping and checking things off of my to help chart

Would some one like to explain why there is no continue in game anymore I'm not going through that again we're is my save 


No I didn't start a new game/ hence the title/ the continue was not there my saves were just gone I had a feeling you would try to blame me / but I did like the game so I decided to start a fresh game but we shouldn't have to go through that after paying for the game

developer_response.body: Hello, we are sorry to hear you are having trouble. Did you or someone happen to start a new game? If you start a new game that is where it will continue from, which unfortunately might be the case here.

reviews.review_description: cute game, not much to see when you first start to play but gets better the more you play, reminds me a lot of the old game Echo which I loved as a kid.

reviews.review_title: Unique and enjoyable if you give it time
reviews.review_description: Playing as two dolphins each with their own powers is unique. It was a little confusing at first, but you get the hang of it. Using echolocation to find stuff is pretty neat. The graphics are OK but I love the music. In terms of story it’s a little shallow to start (no pun intended) but once you reach the deeper levels it gets way more interesting. I like that you can revisit areas once you unlock new powers. Overall I’ve been really enjoying it even if there's not as much action as I thought there would be. If you like exploration types of games or you have ocd and need to find everything you’ll probably get into it, otherwise it might seem a little boring.

reviews.review_title: Just temper expectations for this port..
reviews.review_description:
Jupiter and Mars commits one of entertainments biggest sins in just being rather dull.  That’s not to say it’s bad and does have some cool moments, but what this game boils down too is a repetitive formula for dispatching machines and item hunting, the latter of which is largely there to extend game time.   The Quest 2 port, while addressing some issues from the PSVR release, takes a big graphical hit, removing many of the elements that made it a visual spectacle at times, leaving us with a much less pretty game that can get still gets repetitive.

FULL VIDEO REVIEW - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I2ihkiJ32s4&t=6s

FULL WRITTEN REVIEW - https://www.thevrgrid.com/jupiter-and-mars/
Visually compelling and musically exciting. But it's the message that's so relevant, and it couldn't have come across in a better way.

When I opened the app, I went to the page of the other companies who helped with the game. I watched the video, and got VERY emotional (I’m a big fan of the owners of nature, and the importance of keeping-internet our world safe, and, I live by the ocean as well), and then when I opened the game, I was literally in a state of major awe. Short, sporadic breathing, rigidity, etc. (all good). It was really hard to learn how control the dolphins, and I had no clue I was one, until I read a blurb about the game when I was looking for a control guide. I generally only rate up to four stars, because nothing is ever perfect, but this game gets the coveted fifth star because of its message, beauty, and all around dream product. I have one complaint, though: I wish I could maneuver my FP dolphin with a touch controller, and not have to move my head so much to go up and down. I’m really glad I bought this for my Quest 2, and look forward to playing it more.
Been eagerly awaiting seeing this come to the oculus quest 2!  Such a cool experience, I really dig it a lot!

I’m playing on Quest 2 and the graphics look pretty poor from a technical perspective. The texture maps on some of the sea creatures are extremely low resolution and blocky.  The lighting is flat giving the world an almost 2D appearance.

The movement system feels like driving a camera around. The graphics and the movement system combine to make for an experience that I didn’t find immersive at all.

The first scenario left me wondering what the target demographic is.  It seemed like something for very young children, well under the age of 13, too young for VR.

I like the concept and I like the environments shown in the trailer, but based on what I’ve seen so far I can’t imagine this game delivering anything like what I was expecting.

Update for the developers:

Thanks for the detailed explanation about the graphics.  I also didn’t understand that I was one of the dolphins as you explained in a reply to another review. 

I had refunded the game, but I just repurchased it to give it another chance. I’ll have to give it a lot of time now since I don’t think you can refund twice. :D

It’s definitely getting better…

developer_response.body: Sorry you didn't love what you played thus far, but hopefully you'll enjoy the later levels a lot more where the environments get deeper, you get more abilities, and the story gets more exciting. The first area is admittedly fairly simple, but this was intended to give players some time to get used to controlling both dolphins. Regarding the graphics, we had to keep the poly counts and textures somewhat simple to pull off the massive underwater environments in later stages (pulling this game off on a Quest 2 was no easy feat, trust us!) - but things look much cooler when the game gets deeper and darker. It's a trade-off we made to give players larger areas to explore and to tell the sort of story we wanted to tell. Hopefully you'll venture on further to understand the full experience, but we appreciate you playing it nonetheless!

reviews.review_description: When you discover that you’re the dolphin the game takes on a whole new meaning. So much fun! Exploring and discovering such a great immersive game. Can play it for hours easily.

In my twenties Ecco the dolphin on PS2 really stimulated my imagination. Then, this game came along…..and took me right back there. I can’t tell you how much time I spend here in these waters. I don’t care how glitchy some of the moves are. I don’t care if some of animals are pixelated. Every environment is visually stunning. The background music is nice and mellow. Good job with the underwater sandbox.
This game is worth a buck.  Great idea but lacks any content.  I even tried the free trial and I thought it would have a substantial gaming play.  Sad to say it does not.  Ugh there went my 15 bucks.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear that you didn't find there to be a lot of content. As a lot of other reviewers have pointed out, there's definitely a lot of gameplay once you explore further, including a whole Metroid-vania style progression system. But it requires patience to explore the underwater environments and we know that's not for everyone. That is why we offer it to try for free first. We hope you give it another chance at some point to experience more of the story and content in the game.

reviews.review_description:
I love this game, it's so unique, particularly when you discover that you're a dolphin! You can spend hours exploring the beautiful seascapes, evading drones, and figuring out how to disable generators. When I let my friend try it, she quickly fell in love with it.

My only issue is that of all the treasures I can't find the gold vase in Poseidon. I've looked all around the area it's supposed to be in, according to the guides for the original platform. I can tell that it's there with echolocation, but I can't get close to it. There are unbreakable rocks in the way. Is there a plan to make it accessible to Quest users? Or is there a hidden passage that I'm missing?

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the great review - it means a lot! Regarding the gold vase, thanks for letting us know. It may be a bug. We'll investigate and try to release an update soon if so.

reviews.review_description:
When you first start you think this is a bit iffy but the first bit of level one is more a tutorial..as you go further into the game what a eye opener…the underwater 3d visuals are stunning,as are the sunken buildings ships and landmarks …the game has one hell of a lot of content…I believe those who 
have given low reviews have not given it a chance and have lost out on a great game

reviews.review_title: Very autism friendly and great fun
reviews.review_description:
Writing based on the reaction of my autistic son (mother writing on his account as he’s unable too)

We struggle to find things for Liam to keep his occupied and excited as he’s high functioning but playing this game he is having a blast, the music is very soothing for him abit like a sensory room in his rest bite care.

This game is very important for people with similar problems who struggle in life, VR is a great way to explore the world and new ideas for people who can’t travel.

Highly recommended for everyone at any age based on watching Liam play.

Detail underwater were very lacking, and tasks to do in the game were most times, meaningless. I was very disappointed.  After playing for over an hour I figured my time could be better spent in better quality games. 

Sorry, its a good idea and good story behind it, I just  couldn't play it any longer.

The underworld is very beautiful, and swimming around feels great. 

The only problem (and this is a major problem) is that there is no purpose for the game rather than swimming around.

After 2 hours of swimming, you feel ready to begin the game - Unfortunately there is no game..

Well, I thought it would be nice being under the sea, but they only made it in one way and all you see is one dolphin going around it’s not like I expected at all. It was a total waste of money honestly sorry but I don’t give it any stars And they’re black dolphins with red in them I mean truly what were these people on when they made this? This is the worst I’ve ever seen a child wouldn’t even make it so bad.!!

developer_response.body: We worked hard to create a stylized world, which is why the dolphins have the designs they do. There's a story behind it and the more you play it becomes more clear. There definitely is a learning curve to get the hang of controlling the two dolphins, but once you do there is a whole deep adventure to experience. We understand it's not for everyone, but those who get into the story and the world have really enjoyed it.
